pattern: 3D DP over (house index, last color used, number of neighborhoods formed so far) — transition respects pre-painted houses and accumulates painting cost.
hints: State: dp[i][c][t] = minimum cost to color houses 0..i where house i has color c and there are t neighborhoods so far.
If house i is already painted, only that color is valid; otherwise try all n colors and add cost[i][c-1].
Neighborhoods increment by 1 when house i's color differs from house i-1's color; the final answer is min over all colors of dp[m-1][c][target].

edge_inputs.description: All houses pre-painted, already target neighborhoods
Impossible — existing paint creates more neighborhoods than target
Target 1 — all houses must be same color
Target equals m — every consecutive house must differ

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = size
    n = rng.randint(2, 20)
    target = rng.randint(1, m)
    houses = [rng.choice([0, 0, 0, rng.randint(1, n)]) for _ in range(m)]
    cost = [[rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [houses, cost, m, n, target]
